label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking about the main task of the IARC based on the text provided. First, I need to recall what the IARC does according to the passage. The article mentions that the International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC) is part of the WHO and is responsible for classifying carcinogens into five groups. They're the ones who categorized air pollution as a Group 1 carcinogen. Looking at the options: A) To focus on cancer-causing substances. The IARC does this by classifying carcinogens into groups. They definitely deal with substances that cause cancer. B) To evaluate the air quality. The text says, "Our task was to evaluate the air everyone breathes rather than focus on specific air pollutants," according to Dana Loomis from IARC. So this specific evaluation of air in general, not just specific pollutants, was part of their task in this case. But is this their main task overall, or just part of a study mentioned in the article? C) To study the developing risk of cancer. The IARC is a cancer research agency. They study risks and causes of cancer, including how pollution contributes. Their classification of carcinogens would be part of studying cancer risks. D) To send signals to the international community. The last part of the article says that the report should send a signal to the international community to take action.
